key_insight: A dual-domain ResNet18 can infer crystallographic surface handedness far more reliably from Fermi-surface projection images than from atomic structure images, because relative geometric orientation encodes chirality in both domains.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to automate chirality identification for metal surfaces used in enantiospecific catalysis, sensing, and spintronic/CISS design.
method: Fine-tuned ResNet18 on labeled real-space atomic models to classify chiral surface handedness.
Trained a second ResNet18 on simulated reciprocal-space Fermi-surface projection maps.
Transferred the reciprocal-space model to experimental synchrotron images using minimal fine-tuning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports about 73% classification accuracy on atomic models versus about 99% on Fermi-surface projections, with experimental transfer achieved using only two labeled frames.
watch_item: The strong experimental performance may depend on careful image registration and the availability of representative labeled training examples, especially when extending to new materials or noisy measurements.
paper_title: Decoding Crystallographic Surface Chirality with Machine Learning: From Atomic Geometry to Fermi Surface Projections
